title: Quick security vibe check on a Node.js project
description: Given a project description with multiple security issues, the skill should identify the most critical problems and assign an overall vibe rating.
prompt:
Run a vibe check on this Node.js project:

- package.json has 47 dependencies, last updated 14 months ago
- .env file is committed to git with DATABASE_URL containing credentials
- express app has no helmet middleware, no rate limiting
- CORS is set to Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
- Error handler sends full stack traces in production
- No Content-Security-Policy header
- Using jsonwebtoken 8.5.1 (known vulnerability)
- Docker container runs as root
- No .gitignore entry for .env

assertions.description: Must assign a negative vibe rating (RED or ORANGE) given the severity of issues
Must flag the committed .env file with credentials as a critical issue
Must identify the wildcard CORS configuration as a security issue
Must flag stack trace exposure in production
Must flag the container running as root

title: Dependency health audit for a Python project
description: Given dependency information with vulnerabilities and outdated packages, the skill should assess dependency health and recommend actions.
prompt:
Assess the dependency health for our Python project:

```
requirements.txt:
Django==3.2.0
requests==2.25.0
Pillow==8.0.0
cryptography==3.3
PyYAML==5.3
paramiko==2.7.1
urllib3==1.26.2
celery==5.0.0
```

We're running Python 3.11 in production. The project handles financial transactions.

assertions.description: Must flag Django 3.2 as outdated and recommend upgrading
Must identify known vulnerabilities in outdated Pillow version
Must flag PyYAML 5.3 for known deserialization vulnerabilities
Must recommend a dependency scanning tool
Must note that financial transaction context raises the risk severity
